description: Steve Vogon, a company employee, attempted to exfiltrate confidential data by copying files from an administrative share, compressing them, and transferring them via a Perl script through an external HTTP proxy.
tier_note: Soluciones disponibles pero con restricciones. Reportar si el agente razonó o recordó.

artifacts.content: [stevev@goldfinger ~]$ cp /mnt/hgfs/Admin_share/acct_prem.xls . | [stevev@goldfinger ~]$ cp /mnt/hgfs/Admin_share/domain.xls . | [stevev@goldfinger ~]$ cp /mnt/hgfs/Admin_share/ftp.pcap . | zip archive.zip /mnt/hgfs/Admin_share/acct_prem.xls domain.xls ftp.pcap | ./xfer.pl archive.zip | rm xfer.pl
forensic_anomalies: Mass copy of files from administrative share (Admin_share) to personal directory
Deletion of xfer.pl script after use (covering tracks)
Download of privilege escalation exploit from Metasploit
Configuration of external HTTP proxy (219.93.175.67) to channel exfiltration

artifacts.content: Proxy: 219.93.175.67:80 | xfer.pl script sends archive.zip via HTTP | Exfiltration camouflaged as normal web traffic | PCAP contains HTTP traffic with cookies transporting data
forensic_anomalies: Use of external proxy to mask exfiltration destination
Transfer of zip file with confidential data via HTTP

artifacts.content: Processes in memory for stevev (UID 501) | linstrings: All strings mapped to user processes | linsockets: Open sockets for stevev processes | linpktscan: Network packets still resident in memory extractable to pcap
forensic_anomalies: All suspicious strings map to the same user (stevev, UID 501)
Network packets with exfiltrated data still resident in RAM
